q: The study of plant diseases is called plant (A. pathology B. taxonomy C. physiology D. anatomy). 2025
Biotic diseases are caused by (A. living organisms B. non-living organisms C. pathogenic organism D. unfavorable growing conditions). 2025
Plant disease is any (A. abnormal B. normal C. usual D. complex) condition that alters the appearance or function of a plant. 2025
Plant disease is a (A. geological B. physiological C. ecological D. physical) process that affects some or all plant functions. 2025
Most plant diseases, around (A. 60 B. 75 C. 80 D. 85) percent are caused by fungal or fungal like organisms. 2025
Infectious plant diseases are caused by (A. pathogenic organism B. extremes of temperature C. toxic substances D. essential mineral) 2025
Plant (A. microbiology B. biology C. pathology D. histology) means a study of plant diseases. 2025
Most plant diseases, around 85 percent are caused by (A. nematodes, B. virus, C. fungal, D. mosaics) organisms. 2024
The study of plant diseases is called plant (A. pathology, B. morphology, C. anatomy, D. cytology). 2024
Plant disease is caused by a (A. pet B. insect C. virus D. pathogen) or an environmental factor. 2025
Abiotic diseases are caused by (A. non-living B. living C. biotic D. parasite) environmental condition. 2025
Signs of plant disease are (A. physical, B. chemical, C. geological, D. botanical) evidence of the pathogen. 2026, 2024
Plant pathogens are very similar to those that cause (A. pathogen B. fungi C. disease D. symptom) in humans and animals. 2026
Soft rot of fruits disease caused by (A. Aspergillus B. Botrytis C. Rhizopus D. Fusarium) 2025
(A. Fungi, B. Bacteria, C. Nematodes, D. Viruses) are intracellular pathogenic particles that infect other living organisms. 2024, 2025
Nematodes are simple, (A. unicellular, B. bicellular, C. single cell, D. multicellular) animals. 2024
Oxygen is supplied to the heart muscle by the (A. pulmonary arteries B. pulmonary veins C. coronary arteries D. coronary veins). 2026
Sedentary lifestyle can lead to (A. diabetes B. stroke C. heart D. kidney) diseases. 2026
Examples of non-infectious diseases include (A. cholera B. covid C. diabetes D. tuberculosis). 2025
More white blood cells, lipids and connective tissue build up and harden to form a fibrous plaque called (A. angina B. plaque C. thrombosis D. atheroma). 2025
A stroke is caused by an interruption to the normal blood supply to an area of the (A. heart B. liver C. brain D. pulmonary artery). 2025
Antihypertensives drugs reduce the risk of damage to the kidneys and (A. ears B. eyes C. mouth D. lungs) from high blood pressure. 2025
The myocardial infarction commonly known as a (A. stroke, B. heart attack, C. atheroma, D. paralysis). 2024
Smoking also changes the balance of (A. lipoproteins, B. cholesterol, C. atherosclerosis, D. hypertension) in the blood. 2024
Smoking also changes the balance of lipoproteins in the (A. urine, B. blood, C. bone D. muscle) 2025
The female Anopheles mosquito is therefore a vector of (A. malaria B. cancer C. typhoid D. diarrhoea). 2026
Diseases that are always in populations are described as (A. endanger B. endemic C. semi-endemic D. pandemic). 2025
Most cases of (A. malaria B. typhoid C. Alzheimer's D. cancer) are caused by Plasmodium. 2025
Female Anopheles mosquitoes feed on human blood to obtain the (A. lipid B. vitamin C. protein D. glucose) they need to develop their eggs. 2025
Infectious disease includes (A. diabetes B. tuberculosis C. hypertension D. cardiovascular disease). 2025
Quinine and chloroquine are used in the treatment of (A. tuberculosis B. malaria C. typhoid D. pneumonia). 2025
Artesunate is a drug derived from the plant compound (A. aspirin B. quinine C. mefloquinine D. artemisinin ). 2025
An example of infectious diseases is (A. cardiovascular disease, B. cancer, C. chronic respiratory diseases, D. malaria). 2024
Mosquitoes lay their eggs in(A. soil, B. water, C. leaves, D. blood ). 2024
The best protection against malaria is (A. to drink cleaning water, B. to sleep well, C. to avoid being mosquitoes bitten D. none of these). 2024
